channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Quality of Life & PROs
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Quality of Life & PROs

item.title: Environmentally relevant concentrations of PFDA exacerbate inflammatory bowel disease through impairing macrophagic efferocytosis of apoptotic intestinal epithelial cells.
item.description: Ecotoxicology and environmental safety - Perfluorodecanoic acid (PFDA), a member of the per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) family, is widely used in industrial and consumer products. The compound is bioaccumulative, raising signif...

item.title: Self-Efficacy Mediates the Dyadic Relationship Between Psychological Distress and Self-Care in Individuals With Inflammatory Bowel Disease and Their Caregivers.
item.description: The Psychiatric quarterly - Emotional distress is common among individuals with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and their caregivers, potentially impairing illness management and daily functioning. The psychological mechanis...

item.title: Caregiver burden in inflammatory bowel disease: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: BMC psychology - Caregiver burden is increasingly acknowledged in chronic illnesses but its impact in the context of Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) remains underexplored. A systematic review and meta-analysis wer...

item.title: Fatigue and mood disorders are highly prevalent in patients with inflammatory bowel disease: A single-centre, cross-sectional study using patient-reported outcomes.
item.description: Internal medicine journal - Fatigue is a common and debilitating symptom in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), yet it remains under-recognised in clinical practice. This study aimed to quantify fatigue among Australian patient...

item.title: The role of DAP12 in immune-related inflammatory diseases.
item.description: Inflammation research : official journal of the European Histamine Research Society ... [et al.] - Review - DNAX-activated protein 12 (DAP12) is a key transmembrane adaptor protein containing an immunoreceptor tyrosine-based activation motif. DAP12 associates with a broad spectrum of cell surface recepto...

item.title: Design, synthesis, and evaluation of indole-pyridine chalcone derivatives as novel HK2 inhibitors to modulate macrophage glycolysis and inflammation for IBD treatment.
item.description: European journal of medicinal chemistry - Hexokinase 2 (HK2) promotes M1 macrophage polarization through aerobic glycolysis, so as to increase the release of inflammatory factors and to maintain the pro-inflammatory phenotype. HK2 inhibito...

item.title: Specific Depressive Symptoms are Associated with Subtypes of Fatigue in Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Journal of clinical psychology in medical settings - Fatigue is common in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) yet firm understanding of its management remains unclear. While psychosocial factors influence fatigue, less is known about how these factors d...

item.title: Risk factors of inflammatory bowel disease complicating anemia: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: Medicine - Meta-analysis - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) frequently complicates with anemia, which worsens disease prognosis and quality of life. However, the risk factors for IBD complicating anemia remain unclear. This ...

item.title: Digital health technologies for monitoring and managing IBD-related fatigue: A scoping review.
item.description: Digital health - Review - Fatigue is a common, persistent symptom in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Digital health technologies (DHTs) may enable ongoing monitoring and timely support, yet the relevant evidence remains d...
